verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain. It localizes to the acrosome (intraacrosomal compartment) of sperm cells, not to the outer plasma membrane. The protein appears to be secreted but has no documented stable anchoring to cell-surface TM partners, no covalent surface tethering, and no cell-state-induced or tissue-restricted ecto-presentation. It does not have its own membrane-integration mechanism and is not part of a stably co-trafficked membrane-anchored complex.
